---
bibliography: ../references.bib
---

# Methods

## Data Compilation

The `r species_label` assessment draws on fishery catch and effort,
size-composition, tagging, biological, and abundance-index data. The final data
period currently needs to be confirmed; the report configuration records the
latest data year as `r latest_data_year`.

The `r previous_assessment_year` assessment covered 1952-2021 and excluded the
most recent calendar year available at input preparation because it was
considered incomplete and subject to post-SC revision. It compiled quarterly
catch, effort, length-frequency, weight-frequency, tag-recapture, and
conditional age-at-length data. The current data section should use that
structure as a checklist while replacing every time period, data source,
screening rule, and sampling summary with the accepted `r assessment_year`
inputs.

> **TODO:** Document the final data sources, screening rules, expansion methods,
> and any differences from the `r previous_assessment_year` assessment.

## Fishery Definitions

Model fisheries should be defined by gear, flag or fleet grouping, region,
selectivity group, and tag-recapture group where applicable.

In `r previous_assessment_year`, the 9-region model used 41 fisheries and
separated extraction fisheries from regional longline index fisheries. The
index fisheries were composite longline fisheries used to provide standardised
CPUE information and did not remove catch from the stock. The `r assessment_year`
fishery table should show any changes in gear/fleet definitions, regional
assignment, index-fishery grouping, selectivity grouping, and tag-recapture or
reporting-rate groupings.

> **TODO:** Insert the final fishery definition table from the input-preparation
> workflow.

## Abundance Indices

The abundance-index section should describe nominal and standardised CPUE
series, index fishery definitions, variance treatment, and any covariates or
spatial models used outside the assessment model.

The `r previous_assessment_year` assessment used operational longline data to
derive regional abundance indices with sdmTMB [@anderson_sdmtmb_2022;
@teears_cpue_2023]. That work moved from VAST to sdmTMB after confirming that
the new workflow could closely replicate the previous VAST indices. The final
bigeye CPUE model included hooks between floats, vessel flag, season, depth of
the 15 degrees C isotherm, and the difference between the depths of the
12 degrees C and 18 degrees C isotherms. Region-specific CPUE variance terms
were estimated from log-residuals during model development and then fixed for
later stepwise, diagnostic, and grid models.

> **TODO:** Summarise the final CPUE analysis and link to supporting papers or
> analysis outputs.

## Size Composition and Biological Data

Length-frequency, weight-frequency, and age data should be described with their
filtering, weighting, sample-size treatment, and reporting units.

The previous assessment avoided using length and weight composition
simultaneously for the same information source, weighted longline
weight-frequency samples with respect to flag-specific catch distributions,
halved input sample sizes where the same observations informed both extraction
and index fisheries, and then applied additional down-weighting for the
diagnostic model. Conditional age-at-length observations were used as a
separate likelihood component for growth estimation and were down-weighted
because the otolith samples were not fully independent.

> **TODO:** Add final sample-size summaries and data-weighting settings.

## Tagging Data

When tagging data are included, this section should describe release programs,
recapture processing, tag-reporting groups, tagger effects, mixing assumptions,
and tag likelihood settings.

The `r previous_assessment_year` assessment included RTTP, historical Coral Sea
tagging, PTTP, and Japanese tagging data. Tag releases were adjusted for tag
loss, unusable recaptures, tagger effects, tag shedding, and initial mortality,
and the tag-return likelihood used a negative binomial form. Tag mixing period
was one of the dominant structural-uncertainty axes, so the `r assessment_year`
report should explicitly document any retained or revised mixing-period,
tagger-effect, reporting-rate, and east-of-150 degrees W recapture treatments.

> **TODO:** Confirm the final tagging treatment for the `r assessment_year`
> model.

## Model Description

The assessment model consists of linked components for population dynamics,
fishery dynamics, tagged-fish dynamics, observation models, parameter
estimation, and stock-status interpretation. Technical details of the MFCL
formulation are given by @hampton_spatially-disaggregated_2001 and
@kleiber_multifan-cl_2019.

The population is partitioned into spatial regions and quarterly age classes,
with the oldest age class treated as a plus group. Recruitment is defined as the
appearance of age-class 1 fish in the population and is modelled at quarterly
time steps. A Beverton-Holt stock-recruitment relationship is used primarily so
that yield analyses, projections, and depletion-based reference points can be
calculated [@francis_use_1992; @harley_preliminary_2011]. In WCPO tuna
assessments, the stock-recruitment relationship is generally given a weak
penalty so that it has little influence on estimated historical recruitment
while still supporting reference-point calculations [@ianelli_independent_2012].

Growth, maturity, natural mortality, movement, selectivity, tag-reporting rates,
and fishery catchability are specified or estimated according to the selected
model configuration. The `r previous_assessment_year` assessment estimated
growth internally using conditional age-at-length information, used a Lorenzen
form for natural mortality at age, and adopted a catch-conditioned framework.
Those choices should be checked against the `r assessment_year` model-control
files before final text is written.

The previous model partitioned the population into spatial regions and 40
quarterly age classes, with the oldest age class treated as a plus group and
the model time window running from 1952 to 2021. Recruitment was estimated
quarterly, the final six recruitment deviations were fixed at the geometric
mean, and the Beverton-Holt stock-recruitment relationship was assigned a weak
penalty so that it supported reference-point calculations without dominating
historical recruitment estimates. The current model description should confirm
whether these structural settings, the initial-population treatment, and the
stock-recruitment steepness values remain the same.

> **TODO:** Add final software versions, executable or backend details, model
> time window, regional structure, age classes, parameter phases, control flags,
> and model-control settings used for the selected model set.

## Observation Models and Likelihood Components

The objective function combines likelihood components for fishery data,
abundance indices, size composition, tagging data, conditional age-at-length
data where used, and penalty terms. Catch-conditioned MFCL models treat catch as
known and solve for fishing mortality, reducing the number of effort-deviation
parameters relative to earlier catch-errors models. Index fisheries can then
contribute a CPUE likelihood component.

Size-composition and conditional age-at-length data are usually down-weighted
to account for non-independence and sampling processes that are not fully
represented by the multinomial likelihood. Tag-recapture data are often fitted
with an overdispersed likelihood because tag returns can be more variable than a
simple Poisson process.

For the previous assessment, catch-conditioned models reduced the effort
deviation structure of earlier catch-errors models and allowed CPUE index
fisheries to contribute a direct lognormal likelihood. Size-composition
weighting was treated as an important sensitivity and grid axis, while
Dirichlet-multinomial weighting was explored but not adopted for the diagnostic
model because the preliminary weights degraded abundance-index fits. These
decisions should be revisited against the current CPUE fits, composition fits,
and diagnostics before being carried forward.

> **TODO:** Confirm the final likelihood components, data weights, CPUE
> variances, tag likelihood settings, and any penalties or bounds used in the
> `r assessment_year` model.

## Model Runs

The model-run section should separate diagnostic or base models, stepwise model
development, one-off sensitivities, and the structural uncertainty grid.

The `r previous_assessment_year` assessment adopted extensive jittering and
Hessian checks for the diagnostic model and structural uncertainty grid because
some stepwise models were found to sit at sub-optimal local minima. Grid models
were jittered and the best solution was selected by objective function value,
with positive definite Hessian status treated as desirable but not the only
criterion for management-use models. The `r assessment_year` model-run
registry should therefore include objective function values, gradients, Hessian
status, jitter counts, restart notes, and any evidence that key management
quantities are stable.

> **TODO:** List the final diagnostic model, sensitivity runs, uncertainty axes,
> and model-weighting approach. Model registries are inserted in the Tables
> section when the report is rendered from selected analysis outputs.

## Stock Assessment Interpretation

Dynamic depletion is calculated by comparing estimated spawning potential in
each time period with the spawning potential expected under a no-fishing
scenario, usually written as $SB_t / SB_{F=0,t}$. This approach accounts for
time-varying recruitment in both the fished and unfished trajectories and is
therefore more informative than comparing recent spawning potential with an
initial biomass level that may not be representative.

The WCPFC limit reference point for bigeye tuna is based on 20 percent of
unfished spawning potential. The exact averaging period and recent-period
definitions must be updated for the `r assessment_year` assessment. Fishing
mortality reference quantities are derived from yield analysis, including
$F_{recent}/F_{MSY}$, and dynamic Kobe or Majuro plots can be produced by
repeating the yield calculations through time [@kleiber_multifan-cl_2019].

For comparison, the `r previous_assessment_year` report calculated
$SB_{F=0}$ over 2012-2021, recent spawning potential over 2018-2021, and
recent fishing mortality over 2017-2020. It excluded 2021 from the recent
fishing-mortality average because terminal-year fishing mortality and data
inputs were considered uncertain. The `r assessment_year` report should state
the updated no-fishing reference period, recent-depletion period, recent
fishing-mortality period, and any management objective against which stock
status is interpreted.

> **TODO:** Update the no-fishing reference period, recent-period definition,
> target or management objective text, yield-analysis settings, and projection
> assumptions once the final management-advice workflow is agreed.
